title: The Eight Lingams of Arunachala
subtitle: Arunachala Siva on the Girivalam Path
meta_description: Along the 14 km Girivalam path around Arunachala stand the eight directional lingams — Indra, Agni, Yama, Niruthi, Varuna, Vayu, Kubera, and Esanya — each presided over by a cardinal deity.
body_html:
Lingams — Arunachala Siva on the Girivalam Path

Along the sacred Girivalam path around Arunachala, eight directional lingams mark the cardinal and intercardinal directions, each presided over by a Vedic deity. Pilgrims venerate these shrines during circumambulation as representations of Shiva in his cosmic, all-encompassing form.

The Eight Directional Lingams

  • Indra Lingam — East
  • Agni Lingam — South-East
  • Yama Lingam — South
  • Niruthi Lingam — South-West
  • Varuna Lingam — West
  • Vayu Lingam — North-West
  • Kubera Lingam — North
  • Esanya Lingam — North-East

Additional Lingams

  • Surya Lingam
  • Chandra Lingam
notes: The /lingams source page is an index/hub page with only a hero image and links to individual lingam sub-pages. No prose body content was present beyond the navigation links. Body HTML includes the link list with directional descriptions added for context. Sub-pages (indra-lingam, agni-lingam, etc.) were not fetched as they were not in the task list.
